level_signals: 8+ years of experience in partnerships, business development, enterprise sales, or growth
Expected to build market presence from the ground up in a new geography

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 8+ years of experience in partnerships, business development, enterprise sales, or growth, ideally in a technology or SaaS environment
must_haves.qualifier: ideally in a technology or SaaS environment

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Deep familiarity with the EMEA's business landscape, including enterprise sales cycles, public sector procurement, and the broader London tech ecosystem
Proven track record sourcing, structuring, and closing enterprise or government partnerships in the EMEA
Experience working with or selling to EMEA public sector institutions, large enterprises, or through channel/reseller networks
Strong communicator who can adapt messaging for technical developers, non-technical operators, C-suite executives, and government stakeholders alike
Self-starter with a bias for action; comfortable with ambiguity and excited to build market presence from the ground up
Data-driven mindset — you measure what matters and use insights to iterate quickly
Genuine passion for the future of software creation and democratising access to building software

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Background in developer tools, coding education, SaaS platforms, or digital transformation consulting
Existing enterprise, government, or channel partner relationships in Europe you'd bring to the role
Experience managing or building partner programs at scale, including reseller programs with tiered incentives
Familiarity with European government digital initiatives
Experience expanding into or across broader European markets from a London base

scope.surface: EMEA market — enterprise, government, channel, education, and developer community growth
scope.business_model: B2B and B2G enterprise/government SaaS; channel/reseller distribution; product-led growth in education and developer segments
primary_metrics_inferred: Enterprise and government partnership pipeline and closed deals
EMEA developer and educational institution adoption
Partnership and growth metrics reported to leadership
core_responsibilities: Own end-to-end partnership development with enterprise accounts, government agencies, and public sector institutions in EMEA
Identify, recruit, and activate resellers, system integrators, and channel partners who can distribute Replit's products across EMEA
Build partnerships with EMEA universities, coding bootcamps, and educational institutions to drive adoption of Replit in learning environments
Drive bottom-up adoption among EMEA builders through community engagement, developer relations, and PLG initiatives
Represent Replit at major EMEA technology conferences, trade shows, and industry events
Build and manage a robust deal pipeline, tracking opportunities from prospecting through close
Report on partnership and growth metrics, ensuring activities convert to measurable business impact

green_flags: Clear ownership scope — candidate owns EMEA market presence end-to-end
Covers multiple high-value partnership tracks: enterprise, government, channel, education, PLG
Reports to International Partnerships Lead — structured global team to support
Replit is an established platform with millions of users — not a pre-launch startup
Company explicitly encourages candidates from underrepresented and non-traditional backgrounds
red_flags: Compensation range not disclosed despite role seniority (8+ years required)
Visa sponsorship and work authorisation requirements for UK/EMEA not stated
401(k) with US-only match listed in benefits — suggests US-centric benefits template applied to a London-based role; UK-specific benefits not detailed
No headcount or team context provided — unclear if this is a team of one or joining existing EMEA staff
Broad remit spanning enterprise, government, channel, education, and PLG simultaneously may indicate under-resourcing

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from 8+ years requirement and build-from-scratch mandate)
seniority.manages_people (not stated; inferred false from JD language — no direct reports mentioned)
scope.business_model (inferred from product description and partnership tracks)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not directly quoted)
